input.description: Data-driven UX and product management analysis

test_cases.output_summary: Two-email sequence. Email 1 (220 words): direct subject line 'You haven't opened this in 90 days', no guilt framing, specific edition reference with data point, single CTA, graceful unsubscribe mention. Email 2 (100 words): short final notice, single CTA, no passive aggression. Both sharply voiced.

input.description: Practical farming newsletter for small-scale growers
input.recent_standout: A piece on winter soil amendment timing that generated 40 replies
input.consequence: No hard list clean, just checking in

test_cases.output_summary: Single email (175 words) with unpretentious subject line 'Still farming?', opens with direct acknowledgment without drama, references specific winter soil edition with a reason it matters, single re-engagement CTA, low-pressure tone. Reads naturally for the audience.

input.description: Technology policy newsletter for policy professionals and tech journalists
input.recent_standout: Annotated breakdown of a major AI liability framework
input.newsletter_changes: Added a Friday primary-source briefing for paid subscribers since most of these subscribers joined

test_cases.output_summary: Two-email sequence in precise neutral register. Email 1 leads with what has changed since subscriber joined (new paid Friday briefing) before re-engagement ask. References specific AI liability piece. Subject line is factual not emotional. Email 2 is clean and administratively framed. Both maintain professional tone throughout.
